mnemonics: Think of a 'Thread' like a path (thread) through your code. A single-threaded program is one path; a multi-threaded program has multiple paths running at the same time.
keyPoints: Threads can be created by extending the Thread class or implementing the Runnable interface.
The JVM gives each thread its own call stack for method invocations and local variables.
All threads share the process's resources, like heap memory (where objects live), which requires synchronization.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call the start() method (instead calling run() directly, which doesn't start a new thread).
Accessing and modifying shared data without proper synchronization, leading to race conditions.
